title: hexa-runner Dockerfile curl/wget/ca-certificates patch

trigger.summary: 7/16 bridges FAIL — single root cause: hexa-runner:latest lacks /usr/bin/curl

investigate.verified_via: docker exec hexa-exec sh -c 'which curl' → empty (rc=1)

patch.diff_summary: apt-get install -y line: + curl + wget (joined with existing ca-certificates perl tini); also added comment block citing 2026-04-26 bridge health check root cause
patch.raw_73_admissibility: minimum-add only — curl + wget; preserved all existing packages (ca-certificates, perl, tini); no other Dockerfile change

rebuild.command: docker buildx use hexa-runner-builder && docker buildx build --platform linux/arm64 -f docker/runner/Dockerfile -t hexa-runner:latest --load .
rebuild.duration_s: ~12 (cached layers, only RUN apt + final stages reran)
rebuild.result: DONE — hexa --version + hexa_interp --help both ok inside image
rebuild.scope: host-arch (linux/arm64) only via --load; multi-arch cache build skipped to keep cycle short — re-run tool/build_runner_image.hexa for amd64 cache

verify.container_relaunch: docker stop+rm hexa-exec then docker run -d --name hexa-exec --cpus=4 --memory=4g --memory-swap=4g --pids-limit=512 -v $HOME:$HOME -v /tmp/hexa_escalate:/tmp/hexa_escalate -e HOME=$HOME -w $HOME --entrypoint /bin/sleep hexa-runner:latest infinity
verify.live_probe: oeis.org → http=200 23289 bytes; gwosc.org → http=200 696907 bytes (curl works end-to-end from inside container)

before_after.after_caveat: 2 remaining FAIL are NOT curl-related — they are container OOM (4GB cap)

simbad_bridge.after: PASS — units + sample parse + 4 live targets (Sirius/M31/Sgr A*/Polaris)

openalex_bridge.after: PASS — 14 unit checks + live fetch (3 entries, top_id=W2164731628)

uniprot_bridge.after: PASS accession=P68871 source_url=https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/P42212.json refs=6 all_ok=true — note source_url shows real REST URL, NOT the fallback path; sentinel bug masked because curl now succeeds and ok=true is set the live way

oeis_live_bridge.after: FAIL Killed (rc=137 SIGKILL OOM) — curl returns 23KB data successfully but interp parse OOMs the 4GB container; cgroup memory.events oom_kill=4 confirmed

gw_observatory_bridge.after: TIMEOUT/OOM — curl returns 696KB allevents JSON; interp parse exhausts 4GB before producing output

uniprot_caveat_status: RESOLVED-by-side-effect — sentinel bug exists per prior report (sets ok=false when fetch attempted-and-empty), but curl fix means fetch is no longer empty, so the live path's ok=true assignment runs first and the bug never fires. Bug remains latent — recommend separate fix to mirror gaia pattern (set ok=true source=fallback when fallback fields populated) so uniprot stays green during real network outages.

oom_during_json_parse.evidence: docker exec hexa-exec cat /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.events → oom_kill=4; container cap 4GB; oeis exits rc=137; gw times out at 90s with no stdout
oom_during_json_parse.scope_judgement: OUT-OF-SCOPE for this curl ω-cycle — separate root cause (interp memory profile), needs distinct ω-cycle (raise cap? streaming JSON parse? bridge-side data-window reduction?)
oom_during_json_parse.recommendation: follow-up: investigate hexa interp JSON parser memory footprint OR raise hexa-exec --memory cap (re-evaluate against project_mac_hard_landing_policy 50% cap) OR bridges add response-size guards

tier_1: Dockerfile patch landed: curl + wget + ca-certificates (already present) baked into hexa-runner:latest, host-arch arm64 --load
5/7 previously-FAIL bridges resolved (arxiv, simbad, wikipedia, openalex, uniprot now PASS with live network data)
Bridge health: 9 PASS → 14 PASS out of 16 (9 originally-PASS + 5 resolved); 7 FAIL → 2 FAIL (both OOM, separate failure mode)
tier_2: uniprot sentinel-classification bug now masked but still latent — flagged for separate fix
amd64 multi-arch image cache NOT updated this cycle (host-arch arm64 --load only); next full build via tool/build_runner_image.hexa will refresh both arches
OOM-kill-during-JSON-parse is the next bottleneck for live-fetch bridges with multi-100KB payloads

raw_admissibility.raw_73_minimum_add: PASS — only added curl + wget to existing apt line; no new RUN, no new ARG, no entrypoint change
raw_admissibility.raw_no_break: PASS — image still runs as Linux container with tini PID1 + hexa entrypoint; --version smoke test inside build succeeded; relaunched container preserves all prior cap/bind-mount/env settings
